recap: Belgium mounted an improbable comeback against Senegal, with Romelu Lukaku equalizing in the 92nd minute before Youri Tielemans headed home the leveler just 158 seconds later to force extra time. After Dodi Lukébakio's effort struck the crossbar, VAR intervened to award a contentious penalty for a foul by Lamine Camara, which Tielemans converted in the 125th minute—the latest goal in World Cup history—despite Senegal's goalkeeper Pathé Ciss attempting gamesmanship on the spot. Senegal, who had dominated the match and built a 2-0 lead through Habib Diarra and Ismaïla Sarr, were undone by Belgium's resilience and the controversial VAR decision in a 3-2 defeat.
